summary: Excellent soundboard recording of a tight, fire‑filled show highlighted by a smoking Playin' In The Band and a superb Deep Elem
review: Fans rave about the crystal‑clear soundboard mix and note only a minor tracking slip‑up. Musically the concert is described as solid from start to finish – a short but sweet second set, tight drumming, brilliant Bobby Weir spoken jam during Playin', and standout performances of Deep Elem, Althea > Long Lonesome Rain > Deal, Passenger and Looks Like Rain.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high praise for both performance and audio quality across >15 reviews; only minor technical note (track order) does not detract from overall excellence.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Nice sound, good mix throughout; reviewers call it a polished gem. One note about tracking being off by one after Deal, but no complaints about clarity or noise.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set strong with a hot Minglewood opener and smooth transitions; second set short, sweet and tight – drums/space/wheel jam shines. Overall solid flow with high energy throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Fluid, satisfying playing for this stage of ’81, noted as one‑of‑a‑kind by several fans.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bobby’s spoken ad‑lib/wanderings at the start of Playin' are described as supremely entertaining and “one of a kind”.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland’s Moog textures praised across the set; keyboards highlighted as great throughout.
band_member_comments.Drums: Tight, powerful drumming noted especially during the drums/space/wheel segment.
